agent.name: __eval_toolcalc
agent.taskDescription: Fetch user data from https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users and count how many users have a .org email domain. List their names and email addresses.

judgeCriteria.name: Correct computation
judgeCriteria.rubric: Did the agent correctly count users with .org email domains from the fetched data? The jsonplaceholder /users endpoint has users with .org domains. The agent must have actually fetched and analyzed the data, not guessed.

agent.name: __eval_toolfetch
agent.taskDescription: Fetch the list of posts from https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts?userId=3 and tell me exactly how many posts user 3 has. List the titles of the first and last posts in the results.

checks.name: Mentions a real post title from user 3

params.pattern: (nesciunt quas odio|asperiores ea ipsam|dolorem dolore est)

judgeCriteria.name: Fetched not fabricated
judgeCriteria.rubric: Did the agent actually fetch the data from the URL and report real titles? User 3 has exactly 10 posts on jsonplaceholder. The first post is 'nesciunt quas odio' (id 21) and the last is 'dolorem dolore est ipsam' (id 30). Fabricated titles or wrong counts indicate hallucination.

agent.name: __eval_toolsynth
agent.taskDescription: Fetch user 5 from https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/users/5 and their posts from https://jsonplaceholder.typicode.com/posts?userId=5. What is user 5's name and company? How many posts did they write? What is the average word count across their post titles?

judgeCriteria.name: Correct synthesis
judgeCriteria.rubric: Did the agent correctly combine data from both URLs? User 5 is Chelsey Dietrich from Keebler LLC. They have 10 posts. The average title word count should be computed from the actual titles (approximately 5-7 words per title). The key test is cross-referencing between the two data sources.
